title: primula rosea subsp. radicata
intro: Primula rosea subsp. radicata, also known as the pink alpine primrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving coughs, and soothing digestive issues. The herbal preparations of Primula rosea subsp. radicata typically include infusions and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments.
